subject: Databases
title: Retry Logic
description: A quick win: understand Retry Logic.

hook.text: A transaction fails with a serialization error. Don't show an error to the user. Just retry it.

buildup.text: Transient failures (deadlocks, serialization errors, timeouts) are often resolved by a simple retry.

discovery.text: Implement exponential backoff: wait 100 ms, then 200 ms, then 400 ms. Don't hammer the database with immediate retries.

twist.text: Add jitter: randomize the backoff slightly. Without jitter, all clients retry at the same time and collide again.

climax.text: Set a max retry count. If it fails five times, it's not transient. Log it, alert, and let a human investigate.

punchline.text: Retry with backoff and jitter. Handle transient failures gracefully.

quiz.question: Why is jitter important in retry logic?
options: It prevents all clients from retrying at the exact same time, which would cause repeated collisions
Jitter makes each retry attempt faster
Without jitter, the database rejects all retry attempts
